steps.title: Fit the doubling pattern
steps.content: The bacteria count starts at 44,000, so use $(0,44000)$. Doubling every day means after one day there are 88,000 bacteria, so use $(1,88000)$. Fit $y_1\sim a b^{x_1}$ in Desmos.

steps.title: Read the model
steps.content: Desmos gives $a=44000$ and $b=2$, so the model is $$y=44000(2)^t.$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D has the correct starting value and the doubling factor. Choices A and B use 44,000 as the exponential base, and choice C models halving instead of doubling. Choice D is correct.
